lede: A look at April 21 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Hyein and Jamie Salé, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Indonesian Navy
events.desc: Indonesian Navy submarine KRI Nanggala (402) sinks in the Bali Sea during a military drill, killing all 53 on board.

events.title: 2019 Sri Lanka Easter bombings
events.desc: Eight bombs explode at churches, hotels, and other locations in Sri Lanka on Easter Sunday, killing at least 269.

events.title: Kharkiv Pact
events.desc: The controversial Kharkiv Pact (Russian Ukrainian Naval Base for Gas Treaty) is signed in Kharkiv, Ukraine, by Ukrainian president Viktor Yanukovych and Russian president Dmitry Medvedev; it was unilaterally terminated by Russia on March 31, 2014.

events.title: 1964 New York World's Fair
events.desc: The 1964–1965 New York World's Fair opens for its second and final season.

events.title: Century 21 Exposition
events.desc: The Seattle World's Fair (Century 21 Exposition) opens. It is the first World's Fair in the United States since World War II.

events.title: Brasília
events.desc: Brasília, Brazil's capital, is officially inaugurated. At 09:30, the Three Powers of the Republic are simultaneously transferred from the old capital, Rio de Janeiro.

events.title: United Air Lines Flight 736
events.desc: United Air Lines Flight 736 collides with a United States Air Force fighter jet near Arden, Nevada in what is now Enterprise, Nevada.

events.title: Texas Revolution
events.desc: Texas Revolution: The Battle of San Jacinto: Republic of Texas forces under Sam Houston defeat troops under Mexican General Antonio López de Santa Anna.

events.title: George Washington's reception at Trenton
events.desc: George Washington's reception at Trenton is hosted by the Ladies of Trenton as he journeys to New York City for his first inauguration.

events.title: Wignacourt Aqueduct
events.desc: The Wignacourt Aqueduct is inaugurated in Malta.

births.name: Freddie Dixon
births.line: English motorcycle racer and racing driver (died 1956)

births.name: Manuel Blanco Encalada
births.line: Spanish-Chilean admiral and politician, 1st President of Chile (died 1876)

deaths.text: Kanagaratnam Sriskandan, Sri Lankan-English engineer and civil servant
Rudi Gernreich, Austrian-American fashion designer, created the monokini
Emil Leon Post, Polish-American mathematician and logician
Cosimo I de' Medici, Grand Duke of Tuscany

observances.text: Christian feast day: Holy Infant of Good Health
